label: true-dup
a: BM25 with default k1/b parameters causes one long document (the 19KB review-gate-patterns.md file) to dominate all query results regardless of topic. The fix is to enable length normalisation with k1=1.5 and b=0.75 in retrieval/bm25.py.
b: Discovered that our BM25 implementation was giving disproportionate weight to long documents. A 19 KB review-gate-patterns file scored highest on every query because the default parameters don't normalise for document length. Applied k1=1.5, b=0.75 as a fix.
reviewer_notes: Same observation, different phrasing. The second is clearly a restatement of the first with different word choice. A human reading both in succession would immediately mark them as duplicates.
